words.definition_en: to change your ideas or behaviour so that you can deal with a new situation
words.definition_vi: thích nghi, điều chỉnh để phù hợp với hoàn cảnh mới
words.example: It took me a while to adapt to the new job.

words.definition_en: to change something slightly in order to make it better, more accurate or more effective
words.definition_vi: điều chỉnh (thường là thay đổi nhỏ để chính xác hơn)
words.example: You can adjust the height of the chair.

words.definition_en: if one thing alternates with another, it happens after it and then the other thing happens again in a regular series; happening on one day, week, etc, but not on the one that follows
words.example: He alternates between hope and despair.

words.definition_en: something that you can choose instead of something else; different from something else and able to be used instead of it
words.definition_vi: sự lựa chọn thay thế; mang tính thay thế
words.example: Can you suggest an alternative to this plan?

words.definition_en: to make changes to a document, law, agreement, etc, especially in order to improve it
words.definition_vi: sửa đổi, bổ sung (văn bản, luật lệ)
words.example: The law was amended to include new safety measures.

words.definition_en: not willing to accept much change, especially in the traditional values of society
words.example: My parents are very conservative in their views.

words.definition_en: to change from one system, use or method to another, or to make something do this; to change your beliefs, especially your religious beliefs
words.definition_vi: chuyển đổi; người thay đổi tín ngưỡng
words.example: We're converting the loft into a bedroom.

words.definition_en: to be gradually destroyed as a result of a natural process of change, or a gradual increase in physical or mental weakness
words.definition_vi: phân hủy, thối rữa; sự suy tàn
words.example: The building was allowed to fall into decay.

words.definition_en: to become worse
words.definition_vi: trở nên tồi tệ hơn, xuống cấp
words.example: The weather conditions deteriorated rapidly.

words.definition_en: to change something such as information so that it is no longer true or accurate
words.definition_vi: xuyên tạc, bóp méo (sự thật, âm thanh)
words.example: The speaker distorted the facts to suit his argument.

words.definition_en: continuously changing, growing or developing
words.definition_vi: năng động, sôi nổi, luôn biến đổi
words.example: We need a more dynamic approach to the problem.

words.definition_en: to continue to exist for a long time; to experience and deal with something difficult or painful
words.definition_vi: tồn tại lâu dài; chịu đựng
words.example: The legend has endured for centuries.

words.definition_en: to gradually change and develop over a period of time
words.definition_vi: tiến hóa, phát triển dần dần
words.example: The company has evolved into a global giant.

words.definition_en: to affect the way that someone thinks or behaves; the power to affect the way someone thinks or behaves
words.example: His teacher had a great influence on him.

words.definition_en: a new idea, method or invention
words.definition_vi: sự đổi mới, sáng kiến mới
words.example: Technological innovations are changing our lives.

words.definition_en: using new methods or ideas
words.definition_vi: mang tính đổi mới, sáng tạo
words.example: It was an innovative design that won the award.

words.definition_en: to continue to exist
words.example: The meeting lasted for two hours.

words.definition_en: to make something stay the same
words.example: It's important to maintain a healthy lifestyle.

words.definition_en: to start behaving like an adult and become more sensible as you get older; behaving in the sensible way that you would expect an adult to behave
words.example: He has matured a lot since he started university.

words.definition_en: to change something slightly, especially in order to improve it or to make it less extreme
words.definition_vi: sửa đổi, điều chỉnh (thường để cải thiện)
words.example: We had to modify the plan slightly.

words.definition_en: new or unusual
words.example: It was a novel idea that no one had thought of before.

words.definition_en: to continue to do something or to try to achieve something, even though it is difficult or other people are against it
words.definition_vi: kiên trì, bền bỉ; vẫn tiếp diễn
words.example: If the symptoms persist, you should see a doctor.

words.definition_en: the possibility to develop or achieve something in the future; possible or likely in the future
words.example: The company has the potential to be very successful.

words.definition_en: to develop or improve; the process of developing or improving
words.definition_vi: tiến bộ, phát triển; sự tiến bộ
words.example: I'm making steady progress with my French.

words.definition_en: a radical change or way of doing something is new and very different from the usual way
words.definition_vi: triệt để, quyết liệt, căn bản
words.example: The company needs a radical overhaul.

words.definition_en: to make some small changes in order to improve something
words.example: We need to refine our techniques.

words.definition_en: to improve a situation by correcting things that are wrong or unfair, or make a system work more effectively; a change intended to correct a situation that is wrong or unfair
words.example: The government is planning to reform the prison system.

words.definition_en: to continue to be in a particular situation or condition
words.definition_vi: vẫn còn, duy trì ở một trạng thái
words.example: The situation remains unchanged.

words.definition_en: to change your opinion or judgment of someone or something; to change a piece of writing in order to improve it
words.definition_vi: xem lại, duyệt lại, sửa lại
words.example: I had to revise my estimate after seeing the new data.

words.definition_en: a sudden or major change, especially in ideas or methods; a sudden and great change, especially the violent replacement of a government
words.definition_vi: cuộc cách mạng, sự thay đổi lớn
words.example: The industrial revolution changed the world.

words.definition_en: if an idea, attitude or plan shifts, it changes; a change in an idea, attitude or plan
words.definition_vi: thay đổi, dịch chuyển; sự thay đổi
words.example: There has been a shift in public opinion.

words.definition_en: to affect something in a way that makes it worse, less attractive or less enjoyable
words.example: The rain spoiled our picnic.

words.definition_en: the present situation or the way that things usually are
words.example: Many people want to maintain the status quo.

words.definition_en: to hold something firmly without shaking or moving it; staying at the same level, speed, value, etc
words.definition_vi: giữ vững; ổn định, đều đặn
words.example: He is making steady progress in his recovery.

words.definition_en: to use one thing or person instead of another; something that is used instead of something else
words.example: You can substitute margarine for butter in this recipe.

words.definition_en: to provide the conditions in which something can happen or exist
words.example: The economy is unable to sustain this level of growth.

words.definition_en: to replace one thing with another; a change from one thing to another
words.definition_vi: đổi, tráo đổi; công tắc; sự thay đổi
words.example: I've decided to switch to a different mobile provider.

words.definition_en: to make someone or something completely different
words.example: The internet has transformed the way we work.

words.definition_en: a gradual change or development that produces a particular result
words.example: There is a growing trend towards remote working.

words.definition_en: the same everywhere
words.example: The blocks are of uniform size.

words.definition_en: a sudden solution to a problem, or an exciting new discovery
words.example: Scientists have made a major breakthrough in cancer research.

words.definition_en: able to send different types of communication signals at the same time; a high-speed Internet connection
words.example: Do you have broadband at home?

words.definition_en: to make a computer do something by pressing a button on the mouse
words.example: Click on the icon to open the program.

words.definition_en: with many details or small parts that make it difficult to understand or deal with
words.example: The situation is more complex than it first appears.

words.definition_en: electrical equipment, such as digital cameras, etc, which is bought by the public for their own use
words.example: The company sells a wide range of consumer electronics.

words.definition_en: to make something using a lot of skill; a traditional skill of making things by hand
words.example: Each piece of furniture is carefully crafted by hand.

words.definition_en: information in a form that a computer can use
words.example: The data is stored on a hard drive.

words.definition_en: to move information from a larger computer system to a smaller one; information that has been moved in this way
words.example: You can download the software from our website.

words.definition_en: a set of information on a computer
words.example: I've saved the file to my desktop.

words.definition_en: a small piece of electronic equipment that you connect to a screen, used for playing video games
words.example: I spend too much time playing on my games console.

words.definition_en: operated by people rather than automatically or using computers; a book that gives you instructions about how to do something or how to use something
words.example: Read the instruction manual before you start.

words.definition_en: to connect computers together so that each computer can send and receive information to and from the other computers; a set of computers that are connected to each other
words.example: The computers are all part of the same network.

words.definition_en: relating to energy produced by changing the structure of the central part of an atom
words.example: The country is planning to build more nuclear power stations.

words.definition_en: working on a computer but not connected to the Internet
words.definition_vi: ngoại tuyến, không kết nối mạng
words.example: I prefer to write my reports offline.

words.definition_en: connected to or available through the Internet
words.definition_vi: trực tuyến, có kết nối mạng
words.example: You can buy tickets online.

words.definition_en: someone whose job is to create computer programs
words.example: She works as a computer programmer for a software company.

words.definition_en: something you can use to help you to achieve something, especially something that exists in nature
words.example: The library is a great resource for students.

words.definition_en: a method of doing something using a special skill
words.example: He's learning new techniques for cooking fish.

words.definition_en: to send information from your computer to another computer or the Internet
words.example: I'm uploading the photos to Facebook.

words.definition_en: make a copy of information on your computer; give support to someone by telling other people that you agree with them
words.example: Don't forget to back up your work.

words.definition_en: move things so that they are in different places or positions
words.definition_vi: thay đổi vị trí, sắp xếp lại
words.example: We changed the furniture around to make more space.

words.definition_en: stop being in one state, condition or form and start being in another, or make something do this; take off the clothes you are wearing and put on different ones
words.example: The caterpillar changed into a butterfly.

words.definition_en: take off the clothes or a piece of clothing you are wearing and put on different ones
words.definition_vi: thay bộ đồ khác (cởi bộ đang mặc ra)
words.example: I need to change out of my wet clothes.

words.definition_en: get rid of
words.example: They should do away with school uniforms.

words.definition_en: repair, paint and improve an old building, car, boat, etc; fasten (an item of clothing)
words.definition_vi: trang hoàng, sửa chữa; cài, thắt (quần áo)
words.example: We're going to do up our new house.

words.definition_en: disappear slowly
words.example: The sound of the music faded away into the distance.

words.definition_en: put information into a computer or other electronic machine using keys or a keyboard
words.example: Key in your password to continue.

words.definition_en: change someone or something so that they become something else
words.definition_vi: biến thành, làm cho trở thành
words.example: They've made the old barn into a house.

words.definition_en: put things together without any order; think that one person or thing is another person or thing
words.example: I always mix those two up.

words.definition_en: start/stop a machine/light/etc working
words.example: Can you switch the light off?

words.definition_en: separate an object into pieces
words.example: He took the engine apart to see what was wrong.

words.definition_en: try using something such as a machine or product to find out whether it works correctly or is satisfactory
words.definition_vi: thử nghiệm (xem có hoạt động tốt không)
words.example: We need to test out the new software.

words.definition_en: change or develop into something different; make someone or something change or develop into something different
words.example: The town has turned into a popular resort.

words.definition_en: use all of a supply of something
words.example: We've used up all the milk.

words.definition_en: use something a lot so that it no longer works, or can no longer be used
words.example: The kids are wearing out their shoes.

words.word: a leopard can't change its spots
words.definition_en: used for saying that someone will never change their behaviour or character
words.definition_vi: giang sơn dễ đổi, bản tính khó dời
words.example: I don't think he'll ever stop lying - a leopard can't change its spots.

words.definition_en: all modern conveniences, the machines and pieces of equipment in your house that make life easy and comfortable
words.definition_vi: đầy đủ tiện nghi hiện đại
words.example: The apartment has all mod cons, including a dishwasher and a dryer.

words.definition_en: change a situation by doing something that is very different from what most people usually do or have done in the past
words.definition_vi: phá cách, làm điều khác biệt, phá vỡ khuôn mẫu
words.example: This new design really breaks the mould.

words.definition_en: change your opinion or attitude
words.example: He was against the plan at first, but he's changed his tune now.

words.definition_en: change your opinion about something or decide not to do something you were planning to do
words.example: She was going to sell the house, but she had a change of heart.

words.definition_en: be very familiar with
words.definition_vi: hiểu rõ cái gì tận tường/chân tơ kẽ tóc
words.example: He knows the system inside out.

words.definition_en: waste time and effort trying to do something that someone else has already done well
words.definition_vi: mất công làm những việc mà người khác đã làm tốt rồi
words.example: There's no need to reinvent the wheel - we can use the existing software.

words.definition_en: refuse to change what you are saying or doing despite the opposition or criticism of other people
words.example: He stuck to his guns despite all the criticism.

words.definition_en: the skills and equipment that you need in order to do your job
words.example: A good computer is one of the tools of the trade for a programmer.

words.definition_en: change your life by starting to be a better person or stopping a bad habit
words.definition_vi: cải tà quy chính, bước sang trang mới (thay đổi tốt hơn)
words.example: He's decided to turn over a new leaf and stop smoking.

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.explanation: In this context, 'last' means to continue to exist.
items.sentence: Sometimes I wonder how many of the changes we see around us will really ___ .

items.explanation: A 'shift' in attitudes means a change in opinions or ways of thinking.
items.sentence: There has been a real ___ in attitudes towards this lately.

items.explanation: Mobile phones receiving TV programmes is a new invention/method, hence an 'innovation'.
items.sentence: Mobile phones that receive TV programmes are a real ___ .

items.explanation: 'Potential' refers to the possibility of future success or development.
items.sentence: Iris has a lot of ___ and should do well in her future career.

items.explanation: 'Mature' here means being sensible and using experience.
items.sentence: You have to try to be ___ about the situation and use your experience to find a solution.

items.explanation: 'Switch' means to exchange one thing for another.
items.sentence: This isn't my laptop! Someone must have ___ my bag and theirs.

items.explanation: 'Make progress' is a standard collocation meaning to develop or improve.
items.sentence: We've made a lot of ___ with the plans, but we still have a long way to go.

items.explanation: 'Substitute' means something that is used in place of another.
items.sentence: You might have learnt the theory, but there's no ___ for practical experience.

items.explanation: When organic matter like trees breaks down, it 'decays'.
items.sentence: When trees fall and start to ___ , they provide a home for many insects.

items.explanation: 'Modify' means to make partial changes to improve something.
items.sentence: I've ___ the design by giving the car six wheels instead of four.

exercises.label: A

items.explanation: In the context of a way of life continuing, 'last' is the most appropriate verb.
items.sentence: It seemed to the generation of Britons alive before the Second World War that their way of life would (1) ___ for many years to come.

items.explanation: A 'revolution' refers to a sudden and major change.
items.sentence: However, the war caused a (2) ___ in British life that completely changed the...

items.explanation: 'Status quo' refers to the existing state of affairs.
items.sentence: ...completely changed the (3) ___ .

items.explanation: A 'uniform' society is one where things are the same everywhere.
items.sentence: What had been a fairly (4) ___ society up to that point...

items.explanation: 'Radical' changes are fundamental and extreme.
items.sentence: ...underwent (5) ___ changes after the war.

items.explanation: A 'trend' is a general direction in which something is developing.
items.sentence: The (6) ___ was away from fixed social roles...

items.explanation: A 'dynamic' society is one that is active and constantly changing.
items.sentence: ...towards a more (7) ___ society.

items.explanation: 'Evolve' means to develop gradually over time.
items.sentence: Britain began to (8) ___ into its present form...

items.explanation: 'Novel' means new and unusual/exciting.
items.sentence: ...were at the time (9) ___ and exciting.

items.explanation: 'Innovative' music/fashion uses new ideas.
items.sentence: New and (10) ___ music and fashion...

items.explanation: To have an 'influence' on someone means to affect them.
items.sentence: ...had a great (11) ___ on young people...

items.explanation: 'Conservative' means traditional and resistant to change.
items.sentence: ...and the whole of Britain became less (12) ___ .

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the boxes.

items.explanation: 'Reform' means to improve a system.
items.sentence: It's time we completely ___ the whole prison system.

items.explanation: 'Revise' means to change a report/writing to improve it.
items.sentence: I'd like you to ___ your report to include more information about costs.

items.explanation: 'Distort' means to twist or misrepresent the truth.
items.sentence: I thought the report in the paper completely ___ the truth.

items.explanation: 'Amend' is used for making slight changes to a contract or legal document.
items.sentence: I've ___ the contract and it now says that you will receive 15%.

items.explanation: 'Maintain' means to keep something at a certain level (like balance).
items.sentence: I sometimes find it hard to ___ a balance between work and my home life.

items.explanation: 'Remain' means to continue to be in a certain state (calm).
items.sentence: The important thing is to ___ calm and try to think logically.

items.explanation: 'Adjust' means to move something slightly to make it more comfortable.
items.sentence: You can ___ the chair to make it comfortable by pressing this button.

items.explanation: 'Adapt' means to change behavior to survive or suit new conditions.
items.sentence: This company needs to ___ or we're not going to survive.

items.explanation: 'Alternate Saturdays' means every other Saturday.
items.sentence: Mike and I meet for coffee on ___ Saturdays.

items.explanation: 'Alternative route' means a different route.
items.sentence: There's a lot of traffic, so I'm trying to find an ___ route.

items.explanation: 'Persist' means to continue doing something annoying despite opposition.
items.sentence: You'll regret it if you ___ in annoying people like that.

items.explanation: 'Sustain' means to provide conditions for something to continue.
items.sentence: Can the company ___ the kind of growth we've had recently?

items.explanation: 'Convert' is used for changing religious beliefs.
items.sentence: Many people in Britain ___ to Christianity during the Roman period.

items.explanation: 'Transform' means to change someone/something completely.
items.sentence: Learning to use the Internet has really ___ my life.

items.explanation: 'Spoil' means to ruin an experience.
items.sentence: I hope the bad weather didn't ___ your holiday.

exercises.label: C

exercises.instruction: If the word in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, replace it with one of the words in bold from the other sentences.

items.explanation: A vast 'network' of computers is the correct technical term.
items.sentence: The Internet is really a vast ___ of computers, all connected together.

items.explanation: 'Broadband' is the term for high-speed internet used for watching videos.
items.sentence: Since we got ___ , we've been watching music videos online.

items.explanation: Early games are described as 'primitive' (basic) compared to modern ones.
items.sentence: Early computer games seem quite ___ compared to today's games.

items.explanation: 'Nuclear power' is the correct energy term.
items.sentence: It seems to me that ___ power is far cleaner than oil.

items.explanation: Tools are indeed 'crafted' (made with skill).
items.sentence: These ancient tools have been crafted with an enormous amount of skill.

items.explanation: 'Games console' is the standard term.
items.sentence: The next generation of games ___ will have better graphics.

items.explanation: Beta testing is a 'technique' (method) used in computing.
items.sentence: There's a ___ in computing called 'beta testing', which means you test something to see if it works properly before it becomes official.

items.explanation: A discovery that changes access is a 'breakthrough'.
items.sentence: This latest breakthrough will mean cheaper, faster internet access for all.

items.explanation: Computers 'analyse data'.
items.sentence: The computer has finished analysing all the ___ .

items.explanation: The sea is a natural 'resource'.
items.sentence: The sea is a great natural ___ but we need the right technology to use it.

exercises.label: D

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.explanation: Someone who writes games is a computer programmer.
items.sentence: If you're interested in becoming a computer (1) ___ ...

items.explanation: You read a 'manual' to find out how something works.
items.sentence: ...if you enjoy reading the (2) ___ to find out what's really going on...

items.explanation: 'Consumer electronics' refers to products like TVs.
items.sentence: ...inside consumer (3) ___ like the latest plasma TV...

items.explanation: Getting music from the internet is 'downloading'.
items.sentence: Our six-week course covers everything from (4) ___ music...

items.explanation: Music on a computer is stored in 'files'.
items.sentence: ...(5) ___ from the Internet to writing your own games.

items.explanation: To put your website onto the internet, you 'upload' it.
items.sentence: You'll learn how to (6) ___ your own website to the Internet...

items.explanation: Programming languages involve solving 'complex' problems.
items.sentence: ...and how to solve (7) ___ problems in the latest programming languages.

items.explanation: 'Go online' means to connect to the internet.
items.sentence: Go (8) ___ now and visit us on the net.

items.explanation: 'Click on' is the action of selecting a link.
items.sentence: (9) ___ on 'Opportunities' and start your future now.

items.explanation: 'Offline' means not connected to the internet.
items.sentence: (If for any reason our website is (10) ___ , or you are unable to connect...)

exercises.label: E

exercises.instruction: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in italics. Use the word given in brackets.

items.explanation: 'Change out of' means to take off clothes and put on different ones.
items.sentence: Why don't you ___ those wet things and put something dry on? (out)

items.explanation: 'Fade away' means to disappear slowly.
items.sentence: The image on the screen slowly ___ and I knew it was a computer virus. (away)

items.explanation: 'Test out' means to try something to see if it works.
items.sentence: Let's ___ your new game and see if it works. (out)

items.explanation: 'Back up' means to make a copy of data.
items.sentence: You'll regret it if you don't ___ your important data. (up)

items.explanation: 'Use up' means to finish the entire supply.
items.sentence: Have you recorded on all of ___ the blank CDs I gave you, or have you got some left? (up)

items.explanation: 'Do away with' means to get rid of something.
items.sentence: Did you hear they're thinking of ___ identity cards? (away)

items.explanation: 'Key in' means to enter information using a keyboard.
items.sentence: You need to ___ your details and then press 'enter'. (in)

items.explanation: 'Do up' means to redecorate or improve.
items.sentence: We should ___ Jake's bedroom now he's left home. (up)

exercises.label: F

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

items.explanation: 'Switch on' means to start a machine working.
items.sentence: Could you send someone to have a look at my computer, because nothing happens when I ___ it ___ ?

items.explanation: 'Take apart' means to disassemble into pieces.
items.sentence: I ___ my watch ___ to see what was wrong with it, but I couldn't put it back together again!

items.explanation: 'Change around' means to rearrange positions.
items.sentence: Your essay would make more sense if you ___ the second and third paragraphs.

items.explanation: 'Turn into' means to change or develop into something else.
items.sentence: One of the miracles of nature is the way a caterpillar ___ a butterfly.

items.explanation: 'Wear out' means to use something until it's no longer usable.
items.sentence: I played so much football last season that I ___ two pairs of boots!

items.explanation: 'Turn into' is also used for magical transformations in stories.
items.sentence: In the story, Cinderella's coach ___ a pumpkin at midnight.

items.explanation: 'Mix up' means to put things in the wrong order or confuse them.
items.sentence: My CDs were all in order and now someone's ___ them ___ .

items.explanation: 'Make into' means to change one thing so it becomes another.
items.sentence: I think they've ___ the old library ___ a really nice space for children to read in.

exercises.label: G

exercises.instruction: The phrasal verb do up means something like 'improve'. Put the following phrasal verbs into the sentences and tick the sentences where the phrasal verb means something like 'improve'.

items.explanation: 'Take up' means to start a new hobby. (Doesn't mean improve)
items.sentence: Why don't you ___ yoga?

items.explanation: 'Cheer up' means to become happier, improving your mood.
items.sentence: I was depressed all day, but I started to ___ when I saw Alec. [tick]

items.explanation: 'Brush up' means to improve your knowledge or skill.
items.sentence: I'm going to ___ my French before we go on holiday. [tick]

items.explanation: 'Make up' here means to invent an excuse.
items.sentence: How could you ___ such a ridiculous excuse?

items.explanation: 'Tidy up' means to make a place neat, improving its condition.
items.sentence: If you ___ your room, then you'll be able to find things more easily. [tick]

items.explanation: 'Bring up' means to mention a subject.
items.sentence: Why did you have to ___ the fact that Dennis has lost his job?

items.explanation: 'Dress up' means to wear nice clothes, improving your appearance.
items.sentence: Let's ___ and go out to that new Italian restaurant tonight. [tick]

items.explanation: 'Turn up' means to arrive.
items.sentence: What time did Nigel finally ___ at the party, then?

exercises.label: H

exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.explanation: 'Break with tradition' is a standard idiom.
items.sentence: The government's decision not to publish this report represents a real ___ with tradition.

items.explanation: 'Satisfy the demand' is a common collocation.
items.sentence: Bill Gates made his money by satisfying the ___ for computer operating systems that were easy to use.

items.explanation: 'Know better than to' means to be sensible enough not to do something.
items.sentence: You should ___ better than to spend all your time playing computer games when you've got exams next week.

items.explanation: 'Surf the Web' means to look at different websites.
items.sentence: I was ___ the Web when I came across a site that had lots of information about my favourite band.

items.explanation: You 'click on the link' on a website.
items.sentence: Click on the ___ below to become a member and enjoy everything that Musiconline has to offer.

items.explanation: 'Changed for the worse' is a common phrase.
items.sentence: We've lived in this area for over 20 years, and I can tell you that things have definitely ___ for the worse.

items.explanation: To be 'at/behind the wheel' means to be driving.
items.sentence: When you're ___ the wheel of the new T 4 sports car, it's obvious that there's a lot of computing power...

items.explanation: 'Wheelchair access' refers to the ability for wheelchairs to enter a place.
items.sentence: People's attitudes to disability are changing and more and more places have wheelchair ___ these days.

items.explanation: 'For your own good' means for your benefit.
items.sentence: Now, don't complain about having to spend the holidays away from your computer - it's for your own ___ and you'll thank me later.

items.explanation: To 'throw your energy into' something means to work hard at it.
items.sentence: Since she changed jobs, Anne has really thrown her ___ into her career.

items.explanation: To 'keep up to date' means to have the latest information/versions.
items.sentence: You should keep your computer system up to ___ , or you might find that some programs won't run.

items.explanation: A 'tool kit' is a box or set of tools.
items.sentence: The workman said that he needed a spanner and asked me to pass him one from his ___ .

exercises.label: I

exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.explanation: 'Access to' is the correct preposition.
items.sentence: ...higher education provides access (1) ___ better-paid careers.

items.explanation: 'Changed from... to...' describes a transition.
items.sentence: ...changed in recent decades, (2) ___ one where few people had university degrees to one where they are very common.

items.explanation: 'A good many' is a phrase meaning 'a lot of'.
items.sentence: A (3) ___ many underpaid teachers...

items.explanation: A 'tool box' is where a manual worker keeps their tools.
items.sentence: Swapping the desk for the (4) ___ box...

items.explanation: 'In demand' means much sought after.
items.sentence: ...who are very much (5) ___ demand.

items.explanation: 'Make the change' is the correct verb phrase.
items.sentence: Those who (6) ___ the change...

items.explanation: 'A good deal more' is a phrase meaning 'much more'.
items.sentence: ...can earn a good (7) ___ more than they used to.

items.explanation: 'Know something about' means having knowledge of a subject.
items.sentence: Workers who (8) ___ something about these vital services...

items.explanation: You 'demand fees from' customers.
items.sentence: ...can demand high fees (9) ___ customers...

items.explanation: 'In place of' means instead of.
items.sentence: ...in (10) ___ of the controlled working environment...

items.explanation: 'Make the break' means to separate from a previous situation.
items.sentence: Of course, those who (11) ___ the break know...

items.explanation: 'At a later date' is a standard time phrase.
items.sentence: ...go back to their profession (12) ___ a later date.

exercises.label: J

exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.explanation: Collocations: 'in use', 'of no use', 'what's the use'.
items.sentence:
1. ...system is in ___ .
2. ...it's of no ___ to me...
3. What's the ___ of trying...

items.explanation: Collocations: 'face up to reality', 'in reality', 'reality TV'.
items.sentence:
1. ...faced up to ___ and realised...
2. ...not changed that much in ___ .
3. ...how popular ___ TV has become...

items.explanation: Collocations: 'out of place', 'offered a place', 'take the place of'.
items.sentence:
1. ...not look out of ___ in science fiction films.
2. ...offered a ___ at Oxford...
3. Nothing will ever take the ___ of my old...

items.explanation: Collocations: 'serve a purpose', 'sense of purpose', 'on purpose'.
items.sentence:
1. ...serve a ___ , or are they just for decoration?
2. ...lack a sense of ___ since he retired.
3. ...sent you a computer virus on ___ .

items.explanation: Collocations: 'against the clock', 'watch the clock', 'around the clock'.
items.sentence:
1. ...compete against the ___ to collect...
2. ...watching the ___ until it's time to leave.
3. ...technical support... around the ___ .

items.explanation: Collocations: 'in the form of', 'fill out the form', 'in good form'.
items.sentence:
1. ...in the ___ of the first games consoles.
2. ...fill out the ___ online to become...
3. ...players have been in good ___ lately...

items.explanation: Collocations: 'in the process of', 'peace process', 'process of elimination'.
items.sentence:
1. I was in the ___ of texting Debbie...
2. ...Middle East peace ___ could be restarted.
3. ...it's just a ___ of elimination.

items.explanation: Collocations: 'path leads', 'lead sb to sth (ideas)', 'lead the way'.
items.sentence:
1. The path ___ around the side...
2. What ___ you to come up with...
3. The scientist ___ me into the laboratory...

exercises.label: K

exercises.instruction: Complete each dialogue using the appropriate form of an idiom in the box.

items.explanation: Choosing a woman to run the club would be doing something different from the past.
items.sentence: 'Do you think they might pick Lisa on Thursday?' 'It would really ___ for them to choose a woman to run the club, but I hope so.'

items.explanation: Deciding not to do something planned is a 'change of heart'.
items.sentence: 'I thought you were going to tell Nigel you were angry with him.' 'Well, I ___ and decided to just ignore it after all.'

items.explanation: Starting to behave better is 'turning over a new leaf'.
items.sentence: 'Your son's behaviour in class has improved this term, Mrs Harris.' 'Oh, good. Let's hope he's ___ .'

items.explanation: Doing something already done well by others is 'reinventing the wheel'.
items.sentence: 'Is your research based on other people's work?' 'Some of it. Well, there's no point ___ , is there?'

items.explanation: Refusing to change your mind/position is to 'stick to your guns'.
items.sentence: 'I'm not sure whether to change my mind and let Jenny go to the party.' 'I think you should ___ and show her you're serious.'

items.answer: a leopard can't change its spots
items.explanation: People rarely change their basic character.
items.sentence: 'I wish Terry wouldn't be so thoughtless all the time.' 'I know, but ___ , you know.'

items.explanation: Changing your opinion/attitude is 'changing your tune'.
items.sentence: 'I'm thinking of asking Felicity out on a date.' 'You've ___ , haven't you? You said she annoyed you.'

items.explanation: Necessary equipment for a job are 'tools of the trade'.
items.sentence: 'You've got lots of books on computing languages.' 'It's what I do for a living, so they're just ___ , really.'

items.explanation: 'All mod cons' refers to all modern conveniences in a house.
items.sentence: 'You moved recently, didn't you? What's your new place like?' 'Oh, fantastic. It's got ___ and it's handy for the train.'

items.explanation: Knowing something very thoroughly is knowing it 'inside out'.
items.sentence: 'Maria came round and fixed my computer yesterday.' 'Oh, she ___ computers ___ , doesn't she?'

exercises.label: L

exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.explanation: Adj describing change that never stops.
items.sentence: ...living in a time of (1) ___ (CONTINUE) technological change...

items.explanation: Adj describing a world that is gradually developing.
items.sentence: ...dealing with a constantly (2) ___ (EVOLVE) world of gadgets...

items.explanation: Adj describing changes that are sudden and major.
items.sentence: ...also a time that saw many (3) ___ (REVOLT) changes.

items.explanation: Noun meaning the ability to change or be changed.
items.sentence: People had needed to show (4) ___ (FLEXIBLE) throughout the nineteenth century...

items.explanation: Noun (plural) meaning slight changes.
items.sentence: ...meant constantly making (5) ___ (ADJUST) to deal with...

items.explanation: Adj meaning able to adjust to new conditions.
items.sentence: ...people had to become more (6) ___ (ADAPT) than ever before.

items.explanation: Adj meaning having a lot of influence.
items.sentence: ...and other (7) ___ (INFLUENCE) developments gave people...

items.explanation: Noun meaning the ability to do something.
items.sentence: ...gave people the (8) ___ (CAPABLE) to live and work...

items.explanation: Adj meaning not changed (negated by 'remained').
items.sentence: ...little remained (9) ___ (ALTER) as the camera...

items.explanation: Adj meaning very exciting.
items.sentence: ...all had an (10) ___ (ELECTRIC) effect on people and society.

exercises.label: M

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.explanation: Adj describing weather that changes often.
items.sentence: The weather's been so ___ today that I don't know what to wear. (CHANGE)

items.explanation: Noun form of mature.
items.sentence: Bonnie showed a lot of ___ in the way she handled the problem with Olivia. (MATURE)

items.explanation: Verb meaning to make modern (past participle).
items.sentence: The flats were built in the 1960s, but they were all ___ in the 90s. (MODERN)

items.explanation: Adverb meaning continuing to do something despite rules.
items.sentence: Jack got in a lot of trouble for ___ breaking school rules. (PERSIST)

items.explanation: Noun meaning the ability to endure something difficult.
items.sentence: I really don't think I'd have the ___ to finish a marathon! (ENDURE)

items.explanation: Adj describing food that has been treated/changed.
items.sentence: I feel a lot better since I decided to stop eating ___ food. (PROCESS)

items.explanation: Verb meaning to arrange again or differently.
items.sentence: Can we ___ the lesson for next Wednesday? (ARRANGE)

items.explanation: Adj describing energy sources that can be replaced.
items.sentence: We need to rely more on wave power, wind power and other ___ sources of energy. (NEW)

items.explanation: Noun meaning the process of changing (religion).
items.sentence: I gradually lost touch with Ricardo after his ___ to Islam. (CONVERT)

items.explanation: Adj meaning too valuable to be replaced.
items.sentence: I'm scanning my gran's photos onto my computer because they'd be ___ if anything happened to them. (PLACE)

exercises.label: N
